categories.name: Hematology and Hemostasis
categories.explanation: This category includes parameters related to blood cell counts, hemoglobin levels, and coagulation factors. These measurements help assess overall blood health, oxygen-carrying capacity, and the body's ability to form clots when needed.

categories.name: Carbohydrate Metabolism
categories.explanation: Parameters related to glucose regulation and carbohydrate metabolism. These measurements assess blood sugar control, insulin function, and long-term glycemic status, which are crucial for detecting and managing diabetes and metabolic disorders.

categories.name: Lipid Metabolism
categories.explanation: Lipid profile measurements including cholesterol, triglycerides, and apolipoproteins. These parameters assess cardiovascular risk and lipid metabolism, helping evaluate the risk of heart disease and guide preventive interventions.

categories.name: Proteins
categories.explanation: Protein measurements including total proteins, albumin, and inflammatory markers. These parameters assess nutritional status, liver function, and inflammatory processes in the body.

categories.name: Renal Function
categories.explanation: Parameters that evaluate kidney function and waste elimination. These measurements assess the kidneys' ability to filter blood, maintain electrolyte balance, and eliminate metabolic waste products.

categories.name: Ions
categories.explanation: Electrolyte measurements including sodium, potassium, chloride, and magnesium. These parameters are essential for maintaining fluid balance, nerve function, and muscle contraction throughout the body.

categories.name: Liver Function
categories.explanation: Liver enzyme and bilirubin measurements that assess hepatic health and function. These parameters help detect liver damage, inflammation, and disorders affecting bile metabolism.

categories.name: Iron Metabolism
categories.explanation: Parameters related to iron storage, transport, and utilization. These measurements assess iron status, detect deficiencies or excesses, and evaluate the body's capacity to transport and store iron.

categories.name: Phosphocalcic Metabolism
categories.explanation: Measurements of calcium, phosphorus, and related parameters that assess bone health and mineral metabolism. These parameters are essential for evaluating bone density, parathyroid function, and calcium homeostasis.

categories.name: Bone Markers
categories.explanation: Biochemical markers of bone formation and resorption. These measurements help assess bone turnover, evaluate osteoporosis risk, and monitor bone health, with values that may vary according to menopausal status in women.

categories.name: Acylcarnitine Test
categories.explanation: Carnitine measurements that assess fatty acid metabolism and energy production. These parameters help evaluate metabolic function and detect disorders affecting mitochondrial energy production.

categories.name: Vitamins
categories.explanation: Vitamin and related nutrient measurements including B vitamins, vitamin D, folic acid, and coenzyme Q10. These parameters assess nutritional status and detect deficiencies that can affect various body functions.

categories.name: Immunology
categories.explanation: Immunological parameters assess the immune system's function, including immunoglobulin levels, autoimmune markers, and inflammatory cytokines. These measurements help evaluate immune response and detect potential autoimmune conditions.

categories.name: Endocrinology - Thyroid Hormones
categories.explanation: Thyroid hormone measurements including TSH, free T4, free T3, and reverse T3. These parameters assess thyroid function and help diagnose thyroid disorders that affect metabolism, energy levels, and overall health.

categories.name: Endocrinology - Sex Hormones
categories.explanation: Sex hormone measurements including estradiol, testosterone, and related parameters. These measurements are crucial for evaluating reproductive health, and values may vary significantly according to menstrual cycle phase in women.

categories.name: Endocrinology - Adrenal Hormones
categories.explanation: Adrenal hormone measurements including cortisol and DHEA-S. These parameters assess stress response, adrenal function, and may vary with age. They help evaluate the body's response to stress and detect adrenal disorders.

categories.name: Endocrinology - Pituitary Hormones
categories.explanation: Pituitary hormone measurements including LH, FSH, prolactin, SHBG, ACTH, IGF-1, insulin, and related parameters. These measurements assess reproductive function, growth, and metabolic regulation. Many of these hormones have values that vary according to menstrual cycle phase in women, age, or menopausal status.

categories.name: Preventive Medicine
categories.explanation: Advanced metabolic profiling including omega-3 index and other preventive markers. These measurements provide insights into long-term health risks and nutritional status, supporting personalized preventive medicine approaches.

categories.name: Biochemistry (Other Samples)
categories.explanation: Additional biochemical measurements from alternative sample types that complement standard blood biochemistry analysis.

categories.name: Tumor Markers
categories.explanation: Tumor markers are substances produced by cancer cells or by the body in response to cancer. These measurements aid in cancer screening, diagnosis, and monitoring treatment response.

categories.name: Serology
categories.explanation: Serological tests detect antibodies and antigens related to infectious diseases, helping identify past or current infections and immune status.

categories.name: Urine Analysis
categories.explanation: Urine analysis provides information about kidney function, hydration status, and metabolic waste elimination. Parameters include pH, density, glucose, proteins, and cellular elements.

categories.name: Drug Monitoring and Toxicology
categories.explanation: These measurements assess levels of essential minerals and trace elements, as well as potential toxic substances. They help evaluate nutritional status and detect environmental exposures.

categories.name: Personalized Preventive Medicine Profile
categories.explanation: Advanced metabolic profiling including antioxidant capacity, fatty acid profiles, and oxidative stress markers. These measurements provide insights into long-term health risks and nutritional status.

categories.name: Molecular Biology and Genetics
categories.explanation: Molecular and genetic testing including viral RNA detection, HLA typing, and genetic markers that provide information about disease susceptibility and immune system characteristics.
